profile.name: Google Earth Engine Agent
profile.description: An LLM-planned Google Earth Engine agent that exposes focused remote-sensing and environmental data processing workflows through the standard GAS interface. The model produces a constrained JSON plan, and the agent validates and executes that plan with trusted Earth Engine Python API tools.

provider.name: Geoinformation and Big Data Research Lab (GIBD)

skills.name: Compute NDVI Summary
skills.description: Uses Sentinel-2 or Landsat surface reflectance imagery to compute NDVI over a requested region and date range, then returns mean, minimum, maximum, and standard deviation statistics in the task response plus requested JSON and/or CSV artifacts.

constraints.spatial: Accepts uploaded vector regions, explicit bbox parameters, or natural-language place names. Region priority is: valid vector input dataset first; otherwise parameters.bbox; otherwise place-name lookup/geocoding.
constraints.temporal: Uses the requested date range against the selected Earth Engine image collection.
other: Supported datasets are COPERNICUS/S2_SR_HARMONIZED, LANDSAT/LC08/C02/T1_L2, and LANDSAT/LC09/C02/T1_L2.
Cloud filtering uses dataset metadata fields and a user or model selected cloud percentage threshold.
The LLM plans the workflow, but the agent executes a fixed trusted NDVI tool.

skills.name: Create NDVI Time Series
skills.description: Creates a daily regional NDVI time series from Sentinel-2 or Landsat surface reflectance imagery. For each date with imagery, the agent cloud-masks the collection, builds a daily median NDVI image, reduces it over the requested region, and returns the requested JSON, CSV, and/or HTML line-chart artifacts.

constraints.spatial: Accepts uploaded vector regions, explicit bbox parameters, or natural-language place names. Region priority is: valid vector input dataset first; otherwise parameters.bbox; otherwise place-name lookup/geocoding.
constraints.temporal: Returns one row per date with available imagery in the requested date range.
other: Supported datasets are COPERNICUS/S2_SR_HARMONIZED, LANDSAT/LC08/C02/T1_L2, and LANDSAT/LC09/C02/T1_L2.
When requested, the CSV includes date, contributing image count, mean NDVI, minimum NDVI, maximum NDVI, and NDVI standard deviation.
When requested, the HTML chart visualizes mean daily NDVI over time.

skills.name: Create NDVI Map
skills.description: Creates an NDVI visualization workflow. The preferred preview output is an Earth Engine thumbnail or map tile URL for the actual clipped NDVI raster; the agent also returns a browser-ready HTML Folium map artifact with the Earth Engine NDVI tile layer and analysis boundary.

constraints.spatial: Accepts uploaded vector regions, explicit bbox parameters, or natural-language place names. Region priority is: valid vector input dataset first; otherwise parameters.bbox; otherwise place-name lookup/geocoding.
other: Earth Engine thumbnail/map tile URLs are preview links rather than durable exported files.
The HTML map displays the actual NDVI raster preview from Earth Engine rather than a vector grid approximation.

NDVI map previews relax scene-level cloud metadata filtering and apply per-pixel cloud/shadow/snow masks before median compositing, because strict scene-level filters can remove neighboring satellite tiles and leave partial region coverage.
If the preview is useful, call the export skill to create a durable GeoTIFF raster.

skills.name: Create Cloud-Filtered Composite
skills.description: Builds a cloud-filtered median optical image composite from Sentinel-2 or Landsat and returns processing metadata, Earth Engine thumbnail/map tile preview URLs, and a browser-ready HTML preview wrapping the composite thumbnail. Export task creation is separate and opt-in.

other: Earth Engine thumbnail/map tile URLs are the preferred first delivery mode for visual inspection.
When visualization URLs are available, the agent also writes an HTML artifact for notebook/browser display.

Composite previews relax scene-level cloud metadata filtering and apply per-pixel cloud/shadow/snow masks before median compositing, because strict scene-level filters can remove neighboring satellite tiles and leave partial region coverage.
Preview workflows return Earth Engine visualization URLs and metadata rather than downloading full imagery through GAS.
Export tasks are started asynchronously by Earth Engine and may require follow-up monitoring in Earth Engine or the configured export destination.

skills.name: Create Climate Variable Time Series
skills.description: Creates regional climate and environmental variable time series from Earth Engine collections such as CHIRPS, GRIDMET, Daymet, ERA5-Land, and TerraClimate. Supports precipitation, temperature, humidity, wind, solar radiation, evapotranspiration, soil moisture, and drought-oriented variables where available, returning the requested JSON, CSV, and/or HTML line-chart artifacts.

other: Dataset selection is agentic: GRIDMET is preferred for CONUS daily weather/climate, Daymet for North America daily local climate, CHIRPS for global precipitation-only, ERA5-Land for broad global daily variables, and TerraClimate for monthly climate/water-balance/drought variables.
The agent reduces each image over the requested region and returns one time-series row per image/time step.
Results depend on the selected collection's native spatial/temporal resolution and variable definitions.

skills.name: Summarize Land-Cover Area
skills.description: Computes class area summaries in square kilometers from ESA WorldCover or Dynamic World over the requested region.

other: ESA WorldCover uses the selected static map collection.
Dynamic World summaries use the modal class over the requested date range.
Class area summaries are screening-level outputs and should be reviewed for scale and classification suitability.

skills.name: Create Land-Cover Map
skills.description: Creates a classified land-cover raster preview from ESA WorldCover or Dynamic World and returns an Earth Engine thumbnail artifact, map tile preview metadata, an interactive HTML map, and a class-color legend.

constraints.spatial: Accepts uploaded vector regions, explicit bbox parameters, or natural-language place names. Region priority is: valid vector input dataset first; otherwise parameters.bbox; otherwise place-name lookup/geocoding.
other: ESA WorldCover uses the selected static map collection.
Dynamic World map previews use the modal class over the requested date range.
Earth Engine thumbnail/map tile URLs are preview links rather than durable exported files.
If the preview is useful, call the export skill to create a durable GeoTIFF raster.

skills.name: Create Surface-Water Map
skills.description: Maps surface-water occurrence or recent flood water using JRC Global Surface Water or Sentinel-1 SAR. The agent returns Earth Engine preview metadata, a browser-ready HTML map, CSV regional exposure summary fields such as mapped water area, and GeoJSON water polygons when requested.

constraints.spatial: Accepts uploaded vector regions, explicit bbox parameters, or natural-language place names. Region priority is: valid vector input dataset first; otherwise parameters.bbox; otherwise place-name lookup/geocoding.
constraints.temporal: JRC Global Surface Water occurrence is a long-term occurrence layer. Sentinel-1 flood-water mapping uses the requested date range.
other: Supported datasets are JRC/GSW1_4/GlobalSurfaceWater and COPERNICUS/S1_GRD.
JRC occurrence mapping thresholds the occurrence band; default threshold is 50 percent unless parameters.water_occurrence_threshold is supplied.
Sentinel-1 flood-water mapping thresholds median VV backscatter; default threshold is -17 dB unless parameters.sentinel1_water_threshold_db is supplied.
GeoJSON polygons are generated from the thresholded water mask and are intended for exposure screening, not final flood delineation.
Earth Engine thumbnail/map tile URLs are preview links rather than durable exported files. If a durable raster is needed, request export output or call the export skill.

skills.name: Create Earth Engine Export Task
skills.description: Creates an Earth Engine export task for supported image outputs and returns task id, state, and destination metadata in the GAS response.

other: Google Cloud Storage export is the preferred deployment path for durable raster delivery.
Drive export is available as a fallback.
The export skill polls Earth Engine until the task completes, fails, or reaches wait_timeout_seconds. Use GAS stream or async mode for long-running exports.
For GCS exports, the response includes the gs:// URI, HTTPS object URL, and a signed URL when the deployment can verify the object with Google Cloud Storage credentials.

execute_task.name: ExecuteTask
execute_task.description: Submits a natural-language Google Earth Engine task. The LLM creates a constrained workflow plan; the agent validates the plan and executes trusted Earth Engine Python API tools for NDVI summaries, daily NDVI time series, NDVI maps, optical composites, climate/environmental time series, land-cover area summaries, land-cover maps, surface-water/flood-water maps, or export task creation.

instructions.description: Natural-language GEE task instructions such as 'Compute average NDVI for Centre County, Pennsylvania from Sentinel-2 during June 2024 and return a CSV summary.'

input_datasets.description: Optional vector datasets used as Earth Engine analysis regions. The agent reads the first valid vector input, converts it to WGS84 when needed, and uses its geometry/bounds for all supported workflows by default. Region priority is: valid vector input dataset first; otherwise parameters.bbox; otherwise text place-name lookup/geocoding. Set parameters.use_input_region=false only when you intentionally want to ignore the supplied vector.

artifact_delivery.description: How generated artifacts are delivered in the standard response: 'URL' (default) or 'Encoded'.

primary_artifacts.description: Optional downloadable processing report. Structured GEE processing summary and validated plan metadata are available in the task response under provenance.details.gee_summary and provenance.details.validated_plan.
Summary tables for NDVI, climate/environmental time series, precipitation, land-cover class areas, or surface-water exposure summaries.
Interactive HTML line charts for daily NDVI and climate/environmental variable time series.
Earth Engine visualization URLs and interactive NDVI, optical composite, land-cover, and surface-water map artifacts. Surface-water workflows can also return exposure-ready GeoJSON water polygons.

Raster image outputs such as cloud-filtered composites or NDVI images are produced through Earth Engine export tasks after the client decides a preview is useful. The initial GAS response returns export task metadata; the GeoTIFF is written to the configured Earth Engine export destination after task completion.
Earth Engine export task metadata for long-running raster/table exports, including task id, state, destination, required permissions, and errors when export creation is not allowed.

model.description: Optional model override used for LLM workflow planning. If omitted, the agent default model is used.

bbox.description: Optional explicit bounding box [min_lon, min_lat, max_lon, max_lat]. Used when no valid vector input dataset is supplied; vector inputs override bbox, and bbox overrides text place-name lookup/geocoding.

start_date.description: Optional fallback start date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

end_date.description: Optional fallback end date in YYYY-MM-DD format.

max_cloud_percent.description: Maximum scene-level cloud percentage for Sentinel-2 or Landsat filtering.

scale.description: Earth Engine reduction or export scale in meters. Defaults to the selected dataset resolution.

water_occurrence_threshold.description: For JRC Global Surface Water occurrence mapping, minimum occurrence percentage used to classify a pixel as mapped surface water.

sentinel1_water_threshold_db.description: For Sentinel-1 recent flood/surface-water mapping, VV backscatter threshold in dB; pixels below this value are mapped as water.

variables.description: Optional climate/environmental variables for climate_time_series requests. Examples: precipitation, tmax, tmin, temperature, relative_humidity_max, relative_humidity_min, wind_speed, solar_radiation, pet, aet, soil_moisture, drought_index, vapor_pressure_deficit, snow_water_equivalent.

earth_engine_project.description: Optional Earth Engine project override for deployments that allow it. Most clients should omit this and use the server-managed project.

export_destination.description: Advanced optional override for raster export destination. When GEE_EXPORT_BUCKET is configured, the server defaults to Google Cloud Storage, so ordinary clients should omit this.

gcs_bucket.description: Optional deployment-permitted Google Cloud Storage bucket override. Most clients should omit this and use the server-managed GEE_EXPORT_BUCKET.

wait_timeout_seconds.description: Advanced optional override. Maximum seconds the export skill waits for Earth Engine completion or GCS object availability before returning a timeout status. Ordinary clients should omit this and use the server default.

poll_interval_seconds.description: Advanced optional override. Seconds between Earth Engine/GCS export status checks. Ordinary clients should omit this and use the server default.

signed_url_expiration_seconds.description: For completed GCS exports, requested lifetime of a generated signed HTTPS URL when the deployment has Google Cloud Storage signing credentials.

requested_skill.description: Advanced optional skill-selection hint. Most clients should omit this.

use_input_region.description: Advanced opt-out switch for vector inputs. When input_datasets contains a valid vector, the agent uses it as the analysis region by default. Set false only to ignore the vector geometry and use parameters.bbox or text place-name lookup/geocoding.

credentials.description: The client must provide OPENAI_API_KEY or GIBD_API_KEY for LLM planning. Google Earth Engine credentials are deployment-managed by the GAS server and are not supplied by ordinary clients.

provenance.description: Returns the raw LLM plan, validated constrained plan, GEE processing summary, Earth Engine dataset ids, region/date/scale parameters, tool-call trace, artifact paths, and LLM/tool call counts.

reproducibility.description: Provides dataset ids, date ranges, resolved region geometry/bounds, reducer scale, cloud-filter thresholds, validated plan JSON, geocoding provenance when used, and server-side execution metadata.

validation.description: Validates that the LLM plan uses only supported actions and datasets, resolves to a usable region from place name, input vector, or bbox, uses valid dates, and maps to trusted Earth Engine tool functions.

access_control.description: The client supplies model credentials through the standard GAS credential fields. Earth Engine credentials are server-managed deployment secrets.

operation_policy.description: The LLM is restricted to planning. The agent does not execute arbitrary LLM-generated Python or JavaScript code; it only executes validated, prebuilt Earth Engine Python API tools.

review_policy.description: output_review_required is set to false for demo workflows. Scientific outputs should be reviewed for dataset suitability, scale, and uncertainty before decision use.

governance.policy_notes: The GEE agent is a focused demonstration of GAS exposing Earth Engine as an agentic service, not a general-purpose Earth Engine code execution endpoint.

extensions.description: The Google Earth Engine Agent supports LLM-planned, tool-validated NDVI summaries, daily NDVI time series, NDVI maps, cloud-filtered median composites, climate/environmental time series, land-cover class area summaries, land-cover maps, surface-water/flood-water maps, and Earth Engine export task creation.

data_sources.name: TerraClimate Monthly Climate and Water Balance

deployment_credentials.description: Earth Engine authentication is configured on the GAS server, not supplied by ordinary clients.
required_project_roles: roles/earthengine.writer for thumbnail and export task creation
gcs_bucket_roles: roles/storage.objectCreator for Earth Engine export writes
roles/storage.objectViewer for server-side signed URL generation after export completion

agentic_tooling.description: The LLM produces a constrained JSON plan. The agent resolves place names, validates the plan, and executes trusted Earth Engine Python API tools.
